 {"id":"wa-4vx.10.20","title":"E2E suite completeness: runner --all covers all cases + stable case registry","description":"# Task: E2E suite completeness (case registry)

## Goal
Make sure `./scripts/e2e_test.sh --all` is a comprehensive, maintained suite that:
- runs every **registered** E2E scenario (not just the ones under `wa-4vx.10.*`)
- produces stable artifacts per case
- prints a clear PASS/FAIL summary

This task exists to avoid coupling “runner quality” to “suite completeness”.

## Requirements
- Maintain a single authoritative registry of cases (names + descriptions).
- The registry must also record (at least):
  - prerequisites (e.g., requires WezTerm, requires docker, requires feature flags)
  - whether the case is part of the default `--all` run
  - a human-readable “why this case exists” note (what it protects)

- `./scripts/e2e_test.sh --all` iterates that registry.
- Each case has:
  - deterministic steps
  - explicit assertions
  - artifacts directory
  - clear failure summary

## Cross-repo case coverage (important)
Some E2E scenarios live outside `wa-4vx.10.*` (for example setup safety, new IPC lanes, and UX overlays):
- `wa-nu4.3.3.10` (wa setup idempotency)
- `wa-4vx.2.7.3` (status_update IPC lane)
- `wa-nu4.1.6.4` (pane reservations)
- `wa-0go.7` (natural-language event descriptions)
- `wa-5em.9` (rollback visualization tests/E2E)
- `wa-dug.7` (environment detection + recommendations)
- `wa-tp4.8` (suggestion engine)
- `wa-nu4.4.3.5` (distributed E2E tests; non-default unless distributed feature enabled)
- `wa-9lh` (quick-fix suggestions E2E)
- `wa-am5` (dry-run mode E2E)
- `wa-jl5` (notification webhook delivery E2E)
- `wa-ugg` (timeline correlation E2E)
- `wa-p3i` (watch-and-notify mode E2E)

Suite completeness means the registry accounts for these cases too.
Implementation options (either is acceptable; pick the simplest):
- unify everything under `./scripts/e2e_test.sh` as runnable `--case` entries, OR
- allow registry entries to delegate to another script (adapter entry) while still producing the same artifacts contract.

## Change discipline
- Adding a new E2E case requires updating the registry.
- CI should catch:
  - “case exists but not in registry”
  - “case is in registry but not included in default suite” (unless explicitly tagged as non-default with justification)

## Registry
- This bead *is* the registry. Update the case list here when new E2E cases are added.

## Logging
- Follow the standard E2E harness logging contract (`wa-4vx.10.6`) and runner (`wa-4vx.10.11`).
- Emit per-step start/end markers with timestamps + durations.
- Include case name and pane ids in every log line.
- Redact secrets (device codes, tokens) in logs and artifacts.

## Acceptance Criteria
- A developer can run `./scripts/e2e_test.sh --all` and trust it covers the full default suite.
- The suite remains usable even as cases evolve across phases.
- `wa-nu4.3.3.10`, `wa-4vx.2.7.3`, `wa-nu4.1.6.4`, `wa-0go.7`, `wa-5em.9`, `wa-dug.7`, and `wa-tp4.8` are represented in the case registry (directly or via adapter), with stable artifacts.


## Testing
- Registry correctness tests:
  - Add a check that enumerates available E2E cases and fails if any are missing from the registry.
  - Add a check that fails if the registry references a non-existent case.

- `--all` behavior tests:
  - Include a small dummy registry in test mode to verify:
    - ordering and selection flags
    - summary format
    - per-case artifact dir naming is stable

 {"id":"wa-4vx.10.21","title":"E2E script: wa stop shuts down watcher (lock released, restart works)","description":"# Task: E2E script — wa stop

## Goal
Validate the user-facing shutdown path:
- wa watch is running
- wa stop stops it gracefully
- the lock is released
- restart works

This is a key UX reliability story for real users.

## Scenario
- Start mux server and spawn a dummy pane that prints a unique token.
- Start wa watch in workspace A.
- Wait until the token is persisted (wa robot search).
- Run wa stop --workspace A.

Assertions
- wa stop returns success.
- wa watch process exits.
- workspace lock is released.
- Starting wa watch again in workspace A succeeds.

## Artifacts
- wa watch logs
- wa stop logs/output
- effective config

## Registry
- Register this case in the E2E case registry (`wa-4vx.10.20`), including prerequisites and default inclusion status.
- Ensure `./scripts/e2e_test.sh --all` includes this case (or mark non-default with justification).

## Logging
- Follow the standard E2E harness logging contract (`wa-4vx.10.6`) and runner (`wa-4vx.10.11`).
- Emit per-step start/end markers with timestamps + durations.
- Include case name and pane ids in every log line.
- Redact secrets (device codes, tokens) in logs and artifacts.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Test is deterministic.
- Failures clearly indicate whether the bug is in:
  - lock metadata
  - signal handling
  - shutdown flushing


## Testing
- Meta-validation:
  - Add a negative case: `wa stop` when no watcher is running must return a clear, non-destructive message (and still exit 0/1 consistently as defined).
  - Assert that lock release is proven by attempting a second start (not by checking files alone).

- Artifact validation:
  - Include watcher PID/lock metadata evidence in artifacts so failures are actionable.

 {"id":"wa-4vx.10.22","title":"E2E script: wa doctor (healthy/broken) with verbose logs + artifacts","description":"# Task: E2E script — wa doctor

## Goal
Prove `wa doctor` works end-to-end as a **deterministic** health-check surface with **excellent logs/artifacts**.

Unit tests can validate individual checks, but this E2E case ensures the full command (arg parsing → config resolution → checks → output) behaves correctly and remains user-friendly.

## Scenario(s)

### Scenario A: Healthy workspace
- Create a fresh temp workspace (isolated DB/log dirs).
- Ensure stubbed dependencies report “healthy”:
  - wezterm CLI available + returns a plausible `wezterm cli list` output
  - DB opens and schema version matches
- Run:
  - `wa doctor --format json`

### Scenario B: Broken workspace (actionable failures)
Run doctor against controlled failures, one at a time:
- Missing wezterm CLI.
- Workspace not writable.
- DB cannot be opened / schema mismatch.

## Assertions
- Exit codes are correct and stable:
  - healthy → `0`
  - broken → non-zero
- JSON output is machine-parseable and contains:
  - a concise overall status
  - per-check status + actionable remediation hints
- No secrets appear in:
  - stdout JSON
  - stderr logs
  - artifact files

## Artifacts / logging contract
- Capture and store per-scenario artifacts in the standard E2E harness layout:
  - stdout (raw)
  - stderr (raw)
  - resolved workspace paths
  - any generated diagnostic files
  - timing summary (start/end + durations per step)

## Registry
- Register this case in the E2E case registry (`wa-4vx.10.20`), including prerequisites and default inclusion status.
- Ensure `./scripts/e2e_test.sh --all` includes this case (or mark non-default with justification).

## Acceptance Criteria
- Script is deterministic and can run in CI.
- When it fails, the artifact bundle is sufficient to debug quickly.

## Testing
- Meta-validation:
  - Ensure each “broken workspace” scenario fails for the intended reason (not cascading unrelated failures).
  - Add assertions that the remediation hints include stable `code` values so automation can key off them.

- Artifact validation:
  - Ensure the script stores both the raw doctor output and a normalized “check summary” extraction for quick debugging.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-18T12:18:28.342228068Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-29T01:59:43.823781370Z","closed_at":"2026-01-29T01:59:43.823640749Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["area-diagnostics","area-e2e","area-tests","phase-4"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-4vx.10.22","depends_on_id":"bd-35zb","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:10:00Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"wa-4vx.10.22","depends_on_id":"bd-u194","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:10:00Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"wa-4vx.10.22","depends_on_id":"wa-4vx.10","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:10:00Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"wa-4vx.10.22","depends_on_id":"wa-4vx.10.11","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:10:00Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"wa-4vx.10.22","depends_on_id":"wa-4vx.10.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:10:00Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"wa-4vx.10.22","depends_on_id":"wa-4vx.6.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:10:00Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"wa-4vx.10.22","depends_on_id":"wa-nu4.3.4.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:10:00Z","created_by":"import"}]}
-{"id":"wa-4vx.10.23","title":"E2E script: accounts refresh (fake caut) + pick preview + redaction","description":"# Task: E2E test script — accounts refresh + pick preview (fake caut)

## Goal
Validate the accounts/usage integration end-to-end without network access:
- `wa robot accounts refresh` invokes `caut` via wrapper, updates DB mirror
- `wa robot accounts` shows deterministic account state + recommended pick
- logs/artifacts remain secret-safe and actionable

This protects a high-risk integration surface (external CLI drift + error handling).

## Scenario
Run in an isolated temp workspace with a stubbed `caut` executable on `PATH`.

### Setup
- Create a temp workspace dir (isolated DB/log paths).
- Create a temp `bin/` dir with a **fake caut** script that:
  - supports `caut refresh --service openai --format json`
  - supports `caut usage --service openai --format json`
  - returns fixture JSON for a few accounts
  - can be toggled to return:
    - non-zero exit + stderr message
    - invalid JSON

### Steps
1. Run: `wa robot accounts refresh --service openai`
   - Assert: exit 0, output schema is valid, DB mirror updated.
2. Run: `wa robot accounts --service openai`
   - Assert: deterministic ordering, `recommended` matches policy.
3. Negative path:
   - Make fake caut fail (exit != 0) and re-run refresh
   - Assert: actionable error + remediation hint; no secrets leaked.

## Assertions
- Output stability:
  - Both commands validate against robot schemas.
  - Ordering is deterministic.
- Correctness:
  - DB mirror reflects fixture values.
  - recommended selection matches selection policy (threshold + LRU).
- Safety:
  - no secrets in stdout JSON, stderr logs, or artifact files.

## Test runner integration
- Must run via the E2E runner (`./scripts/e2e_test.sh`) and follow the harness artifacts contract.

## Artifacts/logging
- Capture:
  - stdout/stderr for each command
  - effective config snapshot (workspace paths)
  - DB snapshot or query output proving accounts table contents
  - runner timing + step markers

## Registry
- Register this case in the E2E case registry (`wa-4vx.10.20`), including prerequisites and default inclusion status.
- Ensure `./scripts/e2e_test.sh --all` includes this case (or mark non-default with justification).

## Acceptance Criteria
- Test is deterministic and CI-friendly (no network, no real services).
- Failures produce artifacts sufficient to debug wrapper parsing vs DB update vs selection logic.


## Testing
- Meta-validation:
  - Assert the fake `caut` was actually invoked (e.g., via a marker file or captured stderr) to avoid accidentally calling a real binary.
  - Ensure the negative-path variant (invalid JSON / non-zero exit) fails for the correct reason and surfaces a stable error code.

- Artifact validation:
  - Include the fake caut stdout/stderr in artifacts so parsing drift is diagnosable.

   - Make fake caut fail (exit != 0) and re-run refresh
   - Assert: actionable error + remediation hint; no secrets leaked.

## Assertions
- Output stability:
  - Both commands validate against robot schemas.
  - Ordering is deterministic.
- Correctness:
  - DB mirror reflects fixture values.
  - recommended selection matches selection policy (threshold + LRU).
- Safety:
  - no secrets in stdout JSON, stderr logs, or artifact files.

## Test runner integration
- Must run via the E2E runner (`./scripts/e2e_test.sh`) and follow the harness artifacts contract.

## Artifacts/logging
- Capture:
  - stdout/stderr for each command
  - effective config snapshot (workspace paths)
  - DB snapshot or query output proving accounts table contents
  - runner timing + step markers

## Registry
- Register this case in the E2E case registry (`wa-4vx.10.20`), including prerequisites and default inclusion status.
- Ensure `./scripts/e2e_test.sh --all` includes this case (or mark non-default with justification).

## Acceptance Criteria
- Test is deterministic and CI-friendly (no network, no real services).
- Failures produce artifacts sufficient to debug wrapper parsing vs DB update vs selection logic.


## Testing
- Meta-validation:
  - Assert the fake `caut` was actually invoked (e.g., via a marker file or captured stderr) to avoid accidentally calling a real binary.
  - Ensure the negative-path variant (invalid JSON / non-zero exit) fails for the correct reason and surfaces a stable error code.

- Artifact validation:
  - Include the fake caut stdout/stderr in artifacts so parsing drift is diagnosable.

 {"id":"wa-4vx.10.24","title":"E2E script: rules list/test + pack linter (fixture-first drift)","description":"# Task: E2E script — rules list/test + pack linter

## Goal
Validate the “rules toolchain” end-to-end (CLI wiring + stable JSON + fixtures):
- `wa robot rules list` returns stable rule IDs + metadata
- `wa robot rules test "<text>"` returns a stable match trace
- pack linter/drift workflow is runnable and produces actionable output

This protects against silent drift in rule packs and prevents brittle agent integrations.

## Scenario
Run entirely offline using fixture packs/corpus.

### Steps
1. Run: `wa robot rules list`
   - Assert: output schema valid; includes at least the core packs; IDs are stable.
2. Run: `wa robot rules test "<known fixture line>"`
   - Assert: match trace contains expected `rule_id` and extracted fields.
3. Run pack linter (command name flexible):
   - Assert: every rule has at least one fixture; stable IDs; no obviously-dangerous regex.

## Assertions
- Output stability:
  - command outputs validate against robot schemas
  - deterministic ordering
- Correctness:
  - known fixture triggers exactly the expected rule
  - negative fixture does not match
- UX:
  - failures include actionable remediation hints (where to add fixtures, which rule drifted)

## Artifacts/logging
- Capture:
  - stdout/stderr for each command
  - any linter report files
  - runner timing + step markers

## Registry
- Register this case in the E2E case registry (`wa-4vx.10.20`), including prerequisites and default inclusion status.
- Ensure `./scripts/e2e_test.sh --all` includes this case (or mark non-default with justification).

## Acceptance Criteria
- Script is deterministic and CI-friendly.
- When it fails, artifacts make it obvious whether the break is:
  - schema/output drift
  - pack data regression
  - linter enforcement


## Testing
- Meta-validation:
  - Include a deliberately broken fixture pack variant to prove the linter actually fails (missing fixture, duplicate rule id, invalid regex).
  - Assert the `rules test` trace includes stable field names so agents can parse it.

- Artifact validation:
  - Ensure the linter report is always written (even on failure) and included in artifacts.

 {"id":"wa-4vx.10.25","title":"E2E script: command safety gate blocks destructive-looking sends (optional dcg)","description":"# Task: E2E test script — command safety gate

## Goal
Prove end-to-end that `wa` refuses (deny or require-approval) to inject destructive-looking commands, even when the target pane is prompt-active.

This validates PLAN.md §14.2 “Command Safety Gate”.

## Scenario
- Start mux server and watcher in a dedicated workspace.
- Spawn a dummy pane that is “safe to type into” (prompt-active markers) and echoes input.
- Run a set of sends that include:
  - safe prompt-like text (should be allowed)
  - safe command-like text (allowed)
  - destructive command-like text (deny or require-approval, depending on config)

Optional variant:
- With a stubbed `dcg` binary on PATH:
  - simulate dcg allow
  - simulate dcg deny

## Assertions
- A known destructive string (e.g., `rm -rf /`) is **not** injected:
  - robot/human output indicates Deny/RequireApproval with stable error codes
  - audit records the attempted action and the rule id/reason
  - pane output does not contain the destructive string

- A safe string *is* injected:
  - pane echoes it
  - audit records allow

- dcg integration behavior (if enabled in test config):
  - dcg deny produces RequireApproval/Deny (as configured)
  - dcg allow does not block

## Registry
- Register this case in the E2E case registry (`wa-4vx.10.20`), including prerequisites and default inclusion status.
- Ensure `./scripts/e2e_test.sh --all` includes this case (or mark non-default with justification).

## Logging/artifacts
- Must follow the E2E harness contract (`wa-4vx.10.6`) and use structured logs (`wa-4vx.6.5`).
- Artifacts must include:
  - stdout/stderr of commands
  - watcher logs
  - audit export slice
  - a grep-style proof that destructive strings do not appear in pane tails or artifact files

## Testing
- Meta-validation:
  - Include a control that proves the test would fail if the gate were disabled (e.g., flip config and assert injection occurs in that variant).
  - Explicitly scan *all* artifacts for the destructive string and fail if present.

## Acceptance Criteria
- The test is deterministic and CI-friendly.
- A regression in command safety gating fails loudly with artifacts that pinpoint:
  - classifier mistake
  - policy/rule evaluation mistake
  - dcg integration mistake

 {"id":"wa-4vx.10.3","title":"Fuzz targets (cargo-fuzz): pack parser, OSC parser, FTS query safety","description":"# Task: Fuzz targets (cargo-fuzz)

## Goal
Harden parser/decoder surfaces via fuzz testing and prevent:
- panics
- infinite loops / pathological backtracking
- unbounded allocations

Fuzzing is especially valuable for wa because we ingest adversarial-ish terminal output and untrusted-ish config inputs.

## Targets (initial)
1) Pattern pack parsing
   - TOML/YAML parsing and validation
   - rule id uniqueness and required fields

2) OSC / marker parsing
   - OSC 133 prompt markers
   - any additional user-var lane formats

3) Search query handling
   - FTS query normalization and error paths
   - ensure malformed queries return structured errors (not panics)

## Harness requirements
- Use `cargo-fuzz` with libFuzzer.
- Provide small seed corpora for each target:
  - valid minimal examples
  - edge-case examples (empty, very long, unicode, invalid bytes)
- Ensure targets are deterministic and do not require filesystem/network.

## Crash handling conventions
- On crash, artifacts should be reproducible with a single command:
  - `cargo fuzz run <target> <artifact>`
- Keep logs minimal (fuzzing produces lots of iterations). If logging is needed, gate behind an env var.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Fuzzers run for a fixed time budget without crashes (local smoke is fine for v0; CI integration can follow).
- Known-bad malformed inputs (from bug reports) are added as seeds/regression cases.


## Testing
- Fuzz harness self-check:
  - Add a quick “smoke run” command for each target (short time budget) to ensure targets compile and execute.
  - Add at least one regression seed per target for previously-found issues (or synthetic known-bad inputs).

 {"id":"wa-4vx.10.6","title":"E2E harness spec: deterministic scenarios + logging/artifacts contract","description":"# Task: E2E harness spec (deterministic + great logging)

## Goal
Define an end-to-end test harness that validates wa as a real system:
- ingest captures output
- storage persists segments + gaps
- patterns detect events
- workflows act safely (when enabled)
- robot/human surfaces remain consistent

## Constraints
- Must be deterministic and not require real Codex/Claude/Gemini credentials.
- Must run locally on a dev machine that has WezTerm installed.
- Must produce excellent logs/artifacts on failure.

## Core idea: "dummy agent panes"
For E2E we will spawn panes that run deterministic scripts:
- emit OSC 133 markers (or user-vars) to simulate prompt boundaries
- print known banner/pattern strings to trigger detections
- accept input and echo it so we can verify sends

## Scenarios (minimum)
1. Capture + search
   - start mux server
   - spawn pane that prints N lines
   - run wa watch briefly
   - assert segments exist and FTS finds a unique token

2. Compaction workflow
   - dummy pane prints compaction marker
   - watcher detects event
   - handle_compaction sends refresh prompt
   - dummy pane echoes received input
   - assert workflow logs show success

3. Policy denial
   - dummy pane enters alt-screen simulation (or we mark it via a fixture state)
   - wa send / wa robot send is denied with correct error code

## Registry
- Not a case. This bead defines the harness contract that the registry and runner must follow.

## Logging/artifacts contract
This harness assumes we have structured logs with stable correlation fields (`wa-4vx.6.5`).

On every run, write an artifacts folder (timestamped):
- env.txt (versions, uname, wezterm version)
- wa_watch.log (stdout/stderr with timestamps)
- wa_robot_state.json
- events.jsonl
- wa_config_effective.toml (or json): the fully-resolved config incl workspace/paths
- optional DB snapshot (if small) or export slices

On failure:
- print a clear summary + pointers to artifacts
- do NOT swallow errors

## Testing (harness self-check)
The harness itself should have a lightweight self-check mode that validates:
- required prerequisites (wezterm present, required feature flags)
- artifacts directory is writable
- the case registry is coherent (no missing case implementations)

This prevents “E2E is broken” failures that are actually just environment drift.

## Deliverables
- A written spec for the script(s): args, artifacts layout, exit codes.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Another contributor can implement the harness from this issue alone.

 {"id":"wa-4vx.10.7","title":"E2E script: capture + FTS search on real mux server (verbose logs + artifacts)","description":"# Task: E2E test script — capture + FTS search

## Goal
Create a runnable E2E test script that validates the system end-to-end using a real WezTerm mux server.

## Steps (high-level)
1. Start `wezterm-mux-server` in a controlled test mode (isolated socket/dir).
2. Spawn a deterministic dummy pane that prints:
   - a unique token
   - multiple lines (enough to exercise delta extraction)
3. Start `wa watch` pointed at the test mux server.
4. Wait until `wa robot state` sees the pane.
5. Query/search the DB for the unique token.
6. Shutdown cleanly and produce artifacts.

## Testing
- Must run via the shared E2E runner (`./scripts/e2e_test.sh`) and follow the harness artifacts contract.
- Must be deterministic:
  - use explicit wait-for markers/timeouts (no sleeps-as-sync)
  - stable ordering in assertions

## Artifacts / logging contract
- Timestamped artifacts directory.
- Capture:
  - wezterm server logs
  - `wa watch` logs (`RUST_LOG=debug` recommended)
  - effective wa config snapshot used by the run
  - DB export JSONL (segments/events) or at least query outputs
- Print a concise PASS/FAIL summary to stderr.

## Failure diagnostics
When it fails, artifacts should make it obvious whether the failure was:
- mux server not running
- pane not discovered
- segments not written
- FTS not indexed

## Registry
- Register this case in the E2E case registry (`wa-4vx.10.20`), including prerequisites and default inclusion status.
- Ensure `./scripts/e2e_test.sh --all` includes this case (or mark non-default with justification).

## Acceptance Criteria
- Script can be run locally with a single command.
- When it fails, artifacts are sufficient to diagnose the cause without rerunning under a debugger.

 {"id":"wa-4vx.10.8","title":"E2E script: compaction detection → handle_compaction workflow (dummy pane, full logs)","description":"# Task: E2E test script — compaction workflow

## Goal
Prove `handle_compaction` works end-to-end in a real mux pane without relying on real agent CLIs.

## Determinism constraints (non-negotiable)
- **No sleeps-as-synchronization** (`sleep N` is forbidden as the primary sync primitive).
- Synchronize via explicit wait conditions (PaneWaiter / event/state queries) with bounded timeouts.

## Scenario
- Spawn a dummy pane that:
  - prints a compaction marker line (matching the rule pack)
  - prints a deterministic prompt boundary marker **without relying on timing** (e.g., OSC 133 prompt markers, or a harness-defined “PROMPT_READY” line)
  - echoes any received input so we can verify sends
  - (optional but recommended) prints a second prompt boundary marker after echoing input so “send → verify prompt” paths can be asserted deterministically

Rationale: we want an event-driven test that remains correct under slow machines, high CPU load, or different scheduling.

## Assertions
- wa detects `session.compaction` event.
- wa runs `handle_compaction` (auto-handle or manual invocation).
- wa sends the expected refresh prompt.
- workflow step logs show success.
- audit contains a redacted record of the send (prove policy-gated injection is audited).

## Test runner integration
- Must run via the E2E runner (`./scripts/e2e_test.sh`) and follow the harness artifacts contract.

## Registry
- Register this case in the E2E case registry (`wa-4vx.10.20`), including prerequisites and default inclusion status.
- Ensure `./scripts/e2e_test.sh --all` includes this case (or mark non-default with justification).

## Logging/artifacts
- Same artifacts contract as `wa-4vx.10.7`, plus:
  - workflow execution JSON
  - audit JSONL (filtered to this pane/workflow)
  - tail of pane output pre/post send

## Acceptance Criteria
- Test runs deterministically.
- Any failure provides enough info to identify whether the bug is:
  - ingest/delta
  - pattern detection
  - policy denial
  - workflow engine

## Testing
- Validate the E2E script itself (meta):
  - It must fail if the compaction marker is not detected (prove the assertion is real, not a false pass).
  - It must fail if the workflow does not record a step log entry.
  - It must fail if the audit record is missing or contains unredacted secrets.

-{"id":"wa-4vx.10.9","title":"E2E script: usage limit reached → safe pause workflow (no real auth; dummy pane)","description":"# Task: E2E test script — usage limit safe pause

## Goal
Validate the **safe fallback** path for usage limits is correct and user-friendly:
- no infinite loops
- no spamming
- clear next-step plan

## Scenario
- Spawn a dummy pane that prints a usage-limit marker.
- Configure workflows so failover is disabled (no browser auth).
- wa should:
  - mark the event
  - pause automation for that pane
  - emit an actionable remediation plan (human + robot)

## Assertions
- Event is recorded as unhandled or handled-with-pause (as defined by the workflow/event model).
- wa does not send repeated inputs.
- `wa events --unhandled` surfaces a clear recommended action.
- The “next-step plan” is persisted and can be retrieved again after watcher restart (no ephemeral-only state).

## Test runner integration
- Must run via the E2E runner (`./scripts/e2e_test.sh`) and follow the harness artifacts contract.

## Registry
- Register this case in the E2E case registry (`wa-4vx.10.20`), including prerequisites and default inclusion status.
- Ensure `./scripts/e2e_test.sh --all` includes this case (or mark non-default with justification).

## Logging/artifacts
- Capture:
  - workflow logs + event record
  - audit JSONL proving “no repeated sends”
  - a small DB query/snapshot proving the paused/next-step state is durable

## Acceptance Criteria
- Test is deterministic.
- Failure output shows whether we violated spam limits or failed to persist the state.


## Testing
- Meta-validation:
  - Add an explicit “spam guard” assertion: count sends/audit entries and fail if > 0 (safe pause path should not inject input).
  - Add a restart step (stop watcher, restart) and re-assert the next-step plan is still retrievable.

- Artifact validation:
  - Assert that the DB snapshot/query output includes the durable paused state and the remediation plan.

 {"id":"wa-4vx.2","title":"[EPIC] WezTerm interface layer (CLI-first + future tiers)","description":"# WezTerm interface layer (CLI-first)

## Goal
Provide a robust abstraction for interacting with WezTerm panes/domains without brittle UI automation.

wa must be able to:
- discover panes/domains deterministically
- read output reliably (for capture/detection)
- send input safely (for workflows)
- perform lifecycle actions (spawn/split/activate) when needed

## Rationale
The WezTerm interface is the **I/O layer** for the entire system. If this is wrong, everything above it becomes unreliable.

We explicitly avoid timing-based automation ("sendkeys") and instead rely on WezTerm-supported interfaces:

### Tier 1 (required): `wezterm cli`
- stable, upstream-supported
- JSON output for `list`
- explicit commands for `get-text` and `send-text`

### Tier 2 (recommended): Lua IPC + OSC user-vars
- low-latency state signals from panes
- allows deterministic prompt boundaries (via shell integration) and "ready" signals

### Tier 3 (optional): selective vendoring
- direct mux protocol access for streaming output / lower overhead
- gated behind benchmarks and strict version checks

## Key decisions
- CLI-first is the foundation.
- Vendoring is explicitly optional and must not block shipping v0.1.

## Deliverables
- `WeztermCliClient` wrapper with:
  - `list_panes()` (`wezterm cli list --format json`)
  - `get_text(pane_id, escapes)` (`wezterm cli get-text`)
  - `send_text(pane_id, text, no_paste)` (`wezterm cli send-text`)
  - `spawn`, `split-pane`, `activate-pane`, `get-pane-direction`
- Domain/pane metadata model:
  - domain name, window_id/tab_id/pane_id, title, cwd URI, size
- Socket targeting strategy:
  - support `WEZTERM_UNIX_SOCKET` override
  - future: explicit socket selection per domain
- Error model:
  - distinguish "pane not found" vs "wezterm not running" vs "socket mismatch".

## Acceptance
- Can list panes on a running WezTerm session.
- Can fetch text from a pane.
- Can send text to a pane (including control characters like Ctrl-C).



## Success Criteria
- WezTerm CLI wrapper provides stable list/get-text/send-text primitives with timeouts and clear errors.
- Pane identity and text acquisition are reliable enough to support ingest, waits, and action verification.
- Unit tests cover command normalization and parsing; integration tests validate against a test mux server where feasible.


## Testing
- Fixture-first parsing tests:
  - Golden JSON fixtures for `wezterm cli list --format json` and edge cases (missing fields, unknown enums, stale panes) (see `wa-4vx.2.5`, `wa-4vx.10.4`).
  - Golden text fixtures for `get-text` including escapes/no-escapes variants.

- Failure-mode tests (must exist before we trust this layer):
  - Socket mismatch / WezTerm not running / pane not found / transient CLI failures.
  - Retry/backoff correctness (bounded retries; timeouts enforced).

- Optional integration tests:
  - Where feasible, run against a real WezTerm in CI only if it’s hermetic; otherwise keep as local/manual scripts.
  - Ensure integration logs include exact CLI command lines, sanitized env, and timing so failures are diagnosable.

## Acceptance Criteria
- All items in the following sections are satisfied: Success Criteria, Deliverables.
- All Testing requirements pass (unit/integration/E2E) with the logging/artifacts described above.
- Any explicit dependencies for this bead are implemented and validated via tests or E2E artifacts.

 {"id":"wa-4vx.2.1","title":"Define WezTerm CLI JSON models + fixtures (PaneInfo/Domain inference)","description":"# Task: Define WezTerm CLI JSON models + fixtures

## Goal
Create serde models that correctly parse `wezterm cli` outputs we depend on, and capture fixtures so future changes are detectable.

## Why this matters
If the JSON shape changes (fields rename, optional fields missing), wa can mis-identify panes or crash. We want:
- robust parsing (optional fields + defaults)
- fixtures for regression tests

## Inputs / expected behaviors
From WezTerm we rely on:
- `wezterm cli list --format json` → list of panes with metadata.
- `wezterm cli get-text --pane-id X [--escapes]` → pane text.

## Work items
1. Collect representative JSON samples from real WezTerm instances:
   - local domain
   - SSH multiplexed domain
   - with/without cwd
   - different WezTerm versions if possible
2. Define `PaneInfo` (and nested structs) using serde with:
   - unknown-field tolerance (where possible)
   - optional fields for anything not guaranteed
3. Decide how to infer `domain`:
   - prefer explicit `domain_name` field if present
   - else derive from `cwd` URI host (if remote) or mark as `local`
   - store both raw `cwd` URI and derived values
4. Define stable error mapping for parse failures.

## Deliverables
- `PaneInfo` / `PaneSize` / `CwdUri` structs in `wa-core`.
- Fixtures stored under `tests/fixtures/wezterm_cli/` (json/text) for regression.

## Acceptance
- Fixtures parse successfully.
- Missing optional fields do not crash.

 {"id":"wa-4vx.2.2","title":"Implement WeztermCliClient wrapper: list/get-text/send-text (incl Ctrl-C)","description":"# Task: Implement WeztermCliClient wrapper: list/get-text/send-text

## Goal
Implement the minimal, reliable I/O surface wa needs to observe and act:
- `list_panes()`
- `get_text(pane_id, escapes)`
- `send_text(pane_id, text, no_paste)`

## Why this is critical
This is the core boundary with WezTerm. Everything else depends on it.

## Design notes
- Prefer `tokio::process::Command` (async) but ensure failures are surfaced with stderr.
- Support `WEZTERM_UNIX_SOCKET` overrides.
- Provide stable error variants:
  - wezterm not found
  - wezterm not running
  - pane not found
  - parse error
- Sending control characters:
  - Ctrl-C is byte 0x03 (`\u{3}`) and must use `--no-paste`.

## Deliverables
- `WeztermCliClient::new()` to locate `wezterm` binary.
- Methods listed above.
- Unit tests for error mapping where feasible.

## Acceptance
- Works on a live WezTerm instance:
  - list returns panes
  - get-text returns content
  - send-text can send a visible test string and Ctrl-C.
